data.title: Stock Market: Nasdaq Skids As Oil Prices Jump, Google And Tesla Plunge: Weekly Review
data.description: The Nasdaq experienced a significant drop, reaching crucial lows, while the S&P 500 fell below support levels amidst a surge in oil prices. Both Tesla and Google witnessed a decline in their stocks. Despite this, there were instances of strength in the market. This weekly review showcases the impact of the skidding Nasdaq, rising oil prices, and the plummeting stocks of Google and Tesla.

data.title: Is The Stock Market In 'Turtle Mode?' Here's Why Traders Are Ducking For Cover
data.description: Investors are experiencing ongoing turbulence in the stock market as they seek refuge from the uncertainty. The defensive stance taken by traders is evident in the current market conditions. Speculation is rife as to whether the stock market is in a 'turtle mode,' with traders opting to protect their positions. This cautious approach reflects the fluctuating nature of the market, with investors bracing for potential downturns. It is essential for traders to carefully interpret these defensive actions and strategize accordingly in response to the market volatility.

data.title: Meta Launches Seller App To Boost Facebook Marketplace
data.description: Following the announcement of launching a Seller app, Meta stock experienced a slight increase, contrasting with eBay's decline. The Seller app aims to enhance the functionality of Facebook Marketplace.

data.title: ACA Group Recognized by CNBC as One of the World's Top Fintech Companies 2026
data.description: ACA Group, a prominent provider of governance, risk, and compliance solutions in financial services, has been recognized on CNBC's 2026 list of the World's Top Fintech Companies. This year marks the debut of the regulatory technology category on the list, with ACA being one of the distinguished companies acknowledged. The list, created in collaboration with Statista, showcases leading companies in the industry.

data.title: Treasury Yield Breakout Raises S&P 500 Risks As Trump Weighs 'Massive' Attack
data.description: Both the bond and stock markets are currently moving in sync, potentially signaling increased volatility ahead. Treasury yields have surged, posing risks for the S&P 500 as President Trump considers a significant retaliatory action. This correlation between bonds and stocks suggests investors should prepare for a bumpy ride in the financial markets.

data.title: Cathie Wood Adds $50 Million Of Tesla Stock After 15% Drop
data.description: On Thursday, Cathie Wood's ARK Invest purchased Tesla stock in four of its ETFs, despite a nearly 15% decline in share value. This move saw ARK Invest adding approximately $50 million worth of Tesla stock following the drop.
TRUMP MEETS WITH TOP ADVISERS ON IRAN FRIDAY: NYT

Trump was meeting on Friday with top advisers and senior members of his cabinet to decide whether to intensify the military assault against Iran, according to four people briefed on the planned discussion - NYT

data.title: US government acknowledged withholding grants from states not supporting Trump in 2024, affecting billions in energy funding.
data.description: According to court documents, federal officials have confirmed the suspension of funding for energy projects to states, amounting to billions of dollars. The decision to halt funding was reportedly made solely based on the states' support or opposition to President Trump in the 2024 election.

data.description: A recent report indicated that the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to 4.5%, the lowest it has been in the past five years. The report also revealed that the economy created over 250,000 new jobs last month, surpassing expectations. Additionally, the average hourly wage increased by 3.2% compared to the previous year. This positive trend in job creation and wage growth has been attributed to various factors, including increased consumer spending and business investments. Experts believe that the strong performance of the labor market is indicative of a robust economy.
